reasoning: Based on extensive searches, there is no credible reporting indicating that regular US military personnel entered the terrestrial territory of Yemen between March 17, 2025, and the cutoff date of November 9, 2025. While the US (under the Trump administration) launched a highly intensive air campaign against the Houthis in March 2025 (Operation Rough Rider), analysts at the time rated US ground troops as "highly unlikely." Statements from officials emphasized having "ways of conducting sensitive site exploitations without ground troops." The resolution criteria explicitly exclude Special Operations Forces (SOF), military advisors, and contractors—the only typologies of personnel logically plausible for such a theater. With only ~1.5 months remaining in the year and no indication of an impending conventional ground invasion, the probability of regular US troops physically stepping foot on Yemeni soil by December 31, 2025, is extremely low.
